---



---


The SceneManager references a scene file that doesn't exist.

**The Problem:**
- In `scene_manager.gd` (line 20), the scene dictionary has: `"main": "res://scenes/main.tscn"`
- The file `main.tscn` doesn't exist in the `scenes` directory
- The actual main game scene is `game_root.tscn` (as configured in `project.godot`)

**The Fix:**
Update the SceneManager to use the correct scene path. Change line 20 in `fantasy-world-game/scripts/managers/scene_manager.gd`:

```gdscript:fantasy-world-game/scripts/managers/scene_manager.gd
// ... existing code ...
const SCENES = {
	"start_menu": "res://scenes/ui/start_menu.tscn",
	"main": "res://scenes/game_root.tscn"  // Changed from main.tscn to game_root.tscn
}
// ... existing code ...
```

Also update the fallback in `start_menu.gd` (line 437):

```gdscript:fantasy-world-game/scripts/ui/start_menu.gd
// ... existing code ...
func _switch_to_game() -> void:
	# Switch to the main game scene using SceneManager for smooth transition
	if SceneManagerAutoload:
		SceneManagerAutoload.change_scene("main", false) # No extra transition (we already faded)
	else:
		# Fallback if autoload not available
		push_warning("SceneManagerAutoload not available, using direct scene change")
		get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://scenes/game_root.tscn")  // Changed from main.tscn to game_root.tscn
```

After these changes, clicking "Play Game" should load the game scene. The SceneManager was trying to load a non-existent file, causing the transition to fail silently.
